title: InstantiateMsg
description: Message for instantiating the Skip adapter contract on Cosmos Hub

admins.description: The config admins who can update config and manage routes/executors

default_timeout_nanos.description: Default timeout in nanoseconds (e.g., 1800000000000 for 30 minutes)

executors.description: Initial executors who can call ExecuteSwap (can be empty array)

initial_depositors.description: Initial depositor addresses to register during instantiation (can be empty array)

initial_routes.description: Initial route configurations to register (can be empty array)

max_slippage_bps.description: Maximum allowed slippage in basis points (e.g., 100 = 1%)

Binary.description:
Binary is a wrapper around Vec<u8> to add base64 de/serialization with serde. It also adds some helper methods to help encode inline.

This is only needed as serde-json-{core,wasm} has a horrible encoding for Vec<u8>. See also <https://github.com/CosmWasm/cosmwasm/blob/main/docs/MESSAGE_TYPES.md>.

PathHop.description: A single hop in an IBC path (used for both forward and return paths) Used for multi-chain transfers (e.g., Cosmos Hub → Osmosis or Osmosis → Cosmos Hub)

chain_id.description: Chain ID of the destination for this hop (e.g., "cosmoshub-4", "osmosis-1")

channel.description: IBC channel to use for this hop

receiver.description: Receiver address on the next chain (intermediary or final)

SwapOperation.description: A single swap operation (hop in the swap path) Matches Skip Protocol's SwapOperation schema

interface.description: Optional interface specification returned by Coinhall API. Not generated internally; passed through when present in API responses. See: https://github.com/skip-mev/skip-go-cosmwasm-contracts/pull/108#issuecomment-2099460954 And: https://github.com/skip-mev/skip-go-cosmwasm-contracts/blob/main/contracts/adapters/swap/hallswap/src/contract.rs#L364-L367

SwapVenue.description: Swap venue — on Cosmos Hub only cross-chain venues are supported

oneOf.description: Swap on Osmosis (via PFM + wasm hook)

UnifiedRoute.description: Unified route configuration — cross-chain (Osmosis) only on Cosmos Hub

denom_in.description: Input token denom on Cosmos Hub (what the adapter holds)

denom_out.description: Output token denom on Cosmos Hub (what we expect back)

forward_path.description: Forward path from Cosmos Hub to the swap venue Empty for local routes Example: [Cosmos Hub → Osmosis]

operations.description: Swap operations (multi-hop path) Denoms are AS THEY APPEAR ON THE SWAP VENUE (e.g., Osmosis)

recover_address.description: Recovery address on the swap venue (e.g., osmo1...). Will be used as final destination if return_path is empty on cross-chain routes.

return_path.description: Return path back to Cosmos Hub Empty for local routes Example: [Osmosis → Cosmos Hub]
